🌆 Market News Digest
06/04/2026 EST

🔥 Top Stories
• Iran ceasefire talks dominate risk assets — Trump says talks are “going well,” while IMF flags oil/growth risk and Hormuz reopening remains key
• Broadcom plunges, semis drag tech lower — AVGO fell ~15% after revenue miss, pressuring NASDAQ futures and chip peers
• Bitcoin sinks to 4-month low — BTC near $61K on ETF outflows, geopolitics, and crypto-stock weakness
• Jobs data softer than expected — initial claims rose to 225K, reinforcing labor-market cooling ahead of NFP

⛽ Oil & Energy
• Oil slides after Iran peace hopes — Brent/WTI fell >3% as traders priced a possible de-escalation; OPEC held demand outlook steady
• IMF flags energy shock risk — says Iran war has cut production and lifted global oil prices ~3% above April assumptions
• Trump pivots to coal — vowed $700M in coal investment and “historic action” to lower energy prices

📊 Markets & Macro
• Stocks mixed, rate cut/hike path murky — Dow hit a record, while Nasdaq lagged on tech selloff; Daly/Schmid/Bailey stayed hawkish on inflation
• Treasury market steady — bills/notes sold, funding tone orderly; money market assets hit a record $7.894T
• Labor and inflation signals soften — unit labor costs revised down to 1.8%, productivity 0.3%, but Fed officials still see inflation as priority

🌍 Geopolitical
• Israel-Hezbollah clashes flare — sirens, rocket/drone strikes, and withdrawals in south Lebanon keep regional tension elevated
• Russia says diplomacy still open — Putin says Russia is ready for a Ukraine deal; also downplays NATO threat and backs talks with the U.S.
• Iran-Russia-China axis deepens — Tehran/Moscow announced a $25B nuclear MOU; Putin says new energy deals with China are coming

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• Crypto sentiment remains fearful — crypto fear/greed at 12; over half of BTC supply is reportedly at unrealized loss
• Perps and DeFi keep scaling — perp DEX monthly volume rose to $611.6B; Ether.fi allocated $100M to a Plume RWA vault

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• U.S. trade stance stays hardline — USTR says tariff caps will remain in deals with EU/Japan; new 301 probe on overcapacity is coming
• SEC gains disgorgement backing — Supreme Court upheld broad SEC authority to recover illegal profits
• U.S. House drafts AI bill — fresh sign Washington is moving toward formal AI rules; Canada also unveiled an AI strategy
• Pattern day trader rule ends — $25K minimum for stock day trading is being removed

🏢 Corporate
• SpaceX IPO lands with massive pricing — filing points to a ~$75B raise at $135/share, implying a ~$1.75T valuation
• Google/Apple/Meta AI push — Google unveiled new Texas data-center energy investments; Apple to use Nvidia-powered Google chips for Siri; Meta jumped on Instagram Plus rumors
• Lululemon beats but guides lower — solid Q1 sales, but FY outlook and Americas weakness weighed on shares
• Blackstone private credit tightens withdrawals — redemptions capped at 5% amid rising investor pressure
• Quantinuum debuts above IPO, then fades — strong open before slipping back below issue price later in the session

🌆 Market News Digest
[Jun 2–3, 2026 EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• Middle East war risk drives markets — Iran/U.S./Israel exchanges, Kuwait/Bahrain airport attacks, Hormuz blockade threats, and ceasefire/negotiation chatter kept oil and risk assets on edge
• U.S. stocks and bonds sell off on hawkish data — strong ADP/ISM services and Fed hawkish comments pushed equities lower, while rate-cut hopes faded
• Oil spikes on supply shock fears — U.S. crude jumped after a much bigger-than-expected EIA draw; Brent and WTI closed sharply higher

⛽ Oil & Energy
• U.S. EIA crude inventories fell 7.974M bbl; SPR at lowest since Jan 2024, gasoline/distillate builds offset some of the draw
• Brent settled at $97.81 and WTI at $96.02 as Kuwait airport attack and Hormuz risk amplified supply fears
• Aramco said demand is resilient, while Iraq ordered Kurdistan oil ops to resume Thursday

📊 Markets & Macro
• U.S. ADP added 122K jobs and ISM services rose to 54.5, reinforcing resilient growth despite softer employment sub-indexes
• Fed’s Williams/Logan signaled inflation risks remain elevated; Logan said higher rates may be needed later this year
• OECD trimmed 2026 global growth to 2.8% and warned prolonged disruption could trigger recessions

🌍 Geopolitical
• Iran and Israel continued tit-for-tat strikes; U.S. said it struck Iran while CENTCOM protected commercial shipping in Hormuz
• Kuwait and Bahrain reported missile/drone interceptions; Kuwait airport briefly halted flights after damage/injuries
• Trump said Iran is “close to signing,” but also warned the situation could drag on 2–3 weeks

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• Bitcoin fell under $66K and sentiment stayed extremely fearful; Ethereum slipped below $1,800
• Grayscale launched a Hyperliquid ETF; Kalshi added Bitcoin perpetual futures
• Visa, Mastercard and Stripe were tied to a new stablecoin platform

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• U.S. rolled out new copper/steel/aluminum tariffs for June 8, with exemptions for the EU, UK, South Korea and others
• EU unveiled a tech-sovereignty push for cloud, chips and AI, while the UK CMA tightened Google search rules
• USTR doubled down on forced-labor trade actions and said the Turnberry 15% EU tariff deal remains binding

🏢 Corporate
• Alphabet upsized its equity raise to $84.75B; AI usage stats and capex guidance kept Google/AI spending in focus
• Broadcom beat on Q2 results but AI semiconductor revenue guidance looked lighter than expected
• CrowdStrike beat on revenue/EPS and raised outlook; Medtronic also beat and lifted its dividend
